growth_records.medium: Chemically defined medium (CDM) with glucose and lactic acid

up.method: Monod affinity constant K_S; carbon source and electron donor; no volumetric uptake rate reported

growth_records.cite: Sangha JS et al. Advancing dental biofilm models: the integral role of pH in predicting S. mutans colonization. mSphere (2024). DOI:10.1128/msphere.00743-24. [PMC11774048]
growth_records.snippet: Veillonella parvula Lactic acid 0.246 26.9 2.42 ... V. parvula is an obligate anaerobe that consumes lactic acid (carbon source and electron donor) producing acetate and propionate
growth_records.notes: mu_max = 0.246 h-1 (Table 2), K_S = 26.9 mmol/L (=2.42 g/L lactic acid). Doubling time calculated: td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.246 = 2.817 h. Carbon source is lactic acid (not glucose). Kinetic parameters CITED FROM Sangha et al. 2024, Microbiol Spectr, doi:10.1128/spectrum.03713-23 (ref 17). Products (acetate, propionate) qualitative from cited literature. Modeling paper; no new measured rates.
